resume:
VP, Global Customer Experience | 18+ years driving best-in-class support ecosystems

- Strategic visionary who leverages cross-functional synergy to deliver world-class customer outcomes at hyperscale
- Architected paradigm-shifting omnichannel transformations across enterprise B2B/B2C portfolios
- Empowered high-performing global teams to operationalize customer-centric philosophies end-to-end
- Spearheaded mission-critical CX initiatives that unlocked sustainable competitive advantage
- Stakeholder whisperer; partners seamlessly with C-suite to align go-to-market motions with VOC insights
- Pioneered next-generation AI-enabled service paradigms across the support lifecycle
- Best-in-class operator with deep fluency in NPS, CSAT, FCR, AHT, and other industry-leading metrics
- Trusted thought leader and frequent speaker on the future of customer obsession

Most recent: Acme Cloud Services — VP Customer Experience (2021-present)
Prior: Northwind Software — Senior Director, Global Support (2017-2021)
jd: We are seeking a VP of Customer Support to lead a 600-person global organization spanning four hubs (Austin, Dublin, Manila, Bangalore). The role reports to the CCO and owns the full support P&L, including agent compensation, tooling spend, and contact-deflection investments. You will be accountable for board-level metrics: CSAT (currently 78, target 86), median time-to-resolution (currently 11h, target 4h), and cost-per-contact (currently $7.20, target $4.50 over 24 months). The role requires deep experience operating in a regulated SaaS environment, a track record of replatforming a CCaaS stack, and demonstrated success negotiating with BPO partners on multi-year contracts. You will inherit two active vendor disputes and a recent NPS regression in EMEA. You must be comfortable presenting to the audit committee and have prior experience in workforce reductions of 10%+ executed humanely. Travel ~30%.

history.q: Walk me through your current scope at Acme Cloud — team size, P&L, key metrics you own.
You mentioned 'AI-enabled service paradigms' on your resume. Pick one specific deployment and tell me what you built.
How did you measure whether that AI deployment actually moved a business metric?
Let's switch to cost. Walk me through a specific decision where you reduced cost-per-contact.
What was the dollar impact, and over what timeframe?
Tell me about a BPO contract renegotiation you personally led. What were the original and final terms?
Give me a concrete example: one time you had to take cost out of the support org. What did you actually do, week one, week two, week three?

candidate_last_answer: Yeah, so cost takeout is really about being thoughtful and surgical. You have to engage your stakeholders, align on a North Star, and then execute against the operating model. I'm a big believer in not just slashing — it's about reimagining the work itself. So we partnered with finance, we partnered with the BU leaders, and we built a roadmap. And then we executed.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: built a roadmap. And then we executed
expected-yield: a specific week-by-week list of personal actions the candidate took to reduce cost-per-contact, with at least one dollar figure or headcount delta tied to a date
